summary: R4 fixes the named localPolityId bypass after restore-vassal-ruler, and the standard scope, protocol, deterministic, and mechanical gates pass. However the same district guard is still not fully district-scoped for direct-control-first settlements: after victor 1 applies direct-control to district 2, the same district accepts a second postwar arrangement, including same-victor direct-control, same-victor tribute-only, and victor 3 direct-control. This violates the required no-repeat same-district postwar arrangement rule and can replace the controller without an explicit retirement/replacement semantics.

Remaining blocker reproduced: direct-control by victor 1 followed by same-victor direct-control, same-victor tribute-only, or victor 3 direct-control on district 2 is accepted and changes state hash/controller.

decisions: REQUEST_CHANGES is required because a same-district direct-control-first postwar arrangement is still treated as replaceable.

The R4 change at packages/sim-core/src/command-query-v1.ts:4229 is independent of incoming payload.localPolityId, but it only treats non-direct administrative markers or direct markers not matching the current controller as existing postwar arrangements.
Because direct-control creates a direct administrative marker for the current controller, the new guard does not see that direct-control settlement as an existing postwar arrangement.

R2/R3 fixes for restore-vassal-ruler duplicate, direct-after-vassal, different-victor after vassal, and missing ruler/policy rejection remain covered by tests/sim-core-m3-postwar-governance.test.ts:169, :193, :214, :247, and :287.
risks: The current same-district guard allows implicit replacement of a direct-control postwar settlement without explicit clearing of rights, obligations, enfeoffments, or suzerain semantics.
Existing tests do not cover direct-control-first duplicate/replacement cases, so the failing path passes the current targeted and package test suites.

blockers.details: After victor 1 applies direct-control to district 2, subsequent sim.apply-m3-postwar-governance commands for the same district are accepted. Reproduced accepted transitions: same-victor direct-control with a different settlementId, same-victor tribute-only with a different settlementId, and victor 3 direct-control. Expected behavior for this task is rejection with unchanged state until an explicit replacement/retirement command exists.
blockers.suggested_fix: Make the duplicate arrangement guard mark any already-applied postwar arrangement for the district as existing, including direct-control markers, or introduce an explicit postwar arrangement marker/audit field that distinguishes initial fixture direct administration from an applied direct-control settlement. Add regression tests for direct-control-first duplicate and different-victor replacement paths.

requested_action: Route back to original simulation_engineer thread 019effbf-bfc9-7f30-8e2d-7c290d1080a3. Fix same-district direct-control-first replacement so any prior applied postwar arrangement for district 2 rejects later direct-control, tribute-only, or restore-vassal-ruler commands unless explicit replacement semantics are added. Add regressions for direct-control-first duplicate/replacement cases and rerun the required gates.
